name: Epicor ERP REST API
what_it_does: Epicor ERP REST and OData API for manufacturing and distribution enterprise resource planning. Enables AI agents to manage production orders, inventory, purchasing, financials, and supply chain operations across Epicor Kinetic (formerly Epicor ERP), Epicor Prophet 21, and Epicor Eclipse deployments. Epicor specializes in manufacturing, distribution, retail, and automotive verticals.
use_cases: Managing production orders and work-in-progress from manufacturing automation agents
Tracking inventory and warehouse operations from supply chain agents
Processing purchase orders and vendor management from procurement agents
Integrating Epicor financial data into business intelligence workflow agents
not_for: Simple accounting without manufacturing/distribution module needs
Cloud-only SMB deployments (Epicor targets mid-market to enterprise)
best_when: A manufacturing or distribution company using Epicor ERP wants AI agents to automate production scheduling, inventory management, and supply chain operations programmatically.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: ERP operations across production, inventory, and financials can cascade. Epicor self-hosted instances require on-premise API configuration. Test thoroughly in non-production environments.

auth.notes: Epicor uses Basic auth or API key for REST access; newer versions support OAuth 2.0. Authentication configuration varies significantly by Epicor version and deployment (cloud vs on-prem). Epicor Kinetic cloud uses token-based auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise licensing with per-user or per-module pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise perpetual or subscription licensing; pricing negotiated per deployment
pricing.notes: Enterprise software with negotiated pricing. API access included with ERP license.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits depend on deployment (cloud vs on-prem); configured per instance

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Manufacturing ERP operations (production orders, inventory) can cascade across modules
No public MCP server — REST/OData API with version-dependent auth
Multiple Epicor products (Kinetic, Prophet 21, Eclipse) have different APIs — verify which product
On-premise deployments require VPN/network access — not always internet-accessible
API versioning tied to ERP version — upgrades may break integrations
OData query syntax required for complex filtering and relationships
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Full manufacturing ERP access. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. Auth varies by version/deployment. On-prem instances have variable security configuration.

security_score_components.security_notes: Manufacturing ERP financial and operational data. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. Auth varies by deployment. On-prem security varies.
